location.office_time_required: hybrid — time in office combined with work from home, no specific split stated

secondary_archetype: Supply Chain / Hardware Operations Program Manager

level_signals: 10+ years of progressive experience in supply chain program management, NPI, and hardware operations
Cross-functional ownership across Engineering, Operations, and Supply Chain
Acts as primary Supply Chain lead for assigned programs

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of progressive experience in supply chain program management, NPI, and hardware operations
Strong understanding of BOM management, material planning, and procurement processes.
Demonstrated ability to manage cross-functional programs across Engineering, Operations, and Supply Chain.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and risk management skills.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management capabilities.
Ability to travel internationally as needed
Familiarity with supplier compliance frameworks including TISAX, ISO standards, and ethical sourcing requirements.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Automotive, consumer, hardware, or robotic industry experience preferred

scope.surface: End-to-end supply chain execution across assigned programs and product builds including prototype development, NPI, and fleet launches
scope.business_model: B2B — AI software and foundation models for automakers enabling automated driving
core_responsibilities: Act as the primary Supply Chain lead for assigned programs or internal product builds (e.g., prototypes, new product introduction, subassembly)
Own end-to-end material readiness to support program milestones across R&D, Engineering, Operations, and Manufacturing
Translate engineering BOMs and aggregate Technical Program Manager demand into procurement plans
Identify long-lead items and critical path components early in the product lifecycle
Collaborating with Supply Chain Team Leads to ensure direct purchasing, indirect purchasing, and logistics suppliers are meeting compliance requirements
Serve as the primary program manager for upfitter and partner relationships, ensuring alignment across all stakeholders
Define, track, and report on key supply chain performance metrics including, material availability, total cost saving, inventory cycle count accuracy

green_flags: Clear and detailed responsibility breakdown across multiple functional areas
Role described as 'critical cross-functional' — indicates real organizational importance
Explicit inclusion/diversity commitment with accommodations offered
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet all requirements
Hybrid policy with flexible core working hours
red_flags: 10+ years minimum is a high bar that may be inflexible
International travel required but frequency/extent not specified
Office location is Sunnyvale only — limited flexibility for non-Bay Area candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 10+ years requirement and scope of responsibilities)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (partially stated in JD as KPIs; framing as inferred since not all are explicitly listed as primary metrics)
ambiguities: The JD lists 'Familiarity with supplier compliance frameworks including TISAX, ISO standards' under Skills & Qualifications without explicitly labeling it as required or preferred — classified as hard given its unconditional placement in the required skills list, but could be argued as soft given 'familiarity' language.
The JD does not specify how much international travel is required ('as needed'), making it difficult to assess burden on candidates.
